commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Maintainers need to decide the Claude CLI default permission contract and resolve the blocked dependency graph changes before any automated repair would be safe.; Needs attention: The permission hardening is security-relevant and the branch also introduces blocked dependency graph changes that need removal or explicit security/admin approval.; Review finding: [P1] Remove the blocked shrinkwrap deltas — npm-sh... commands.review_summary:
The branch changes Anthropic Claude CLI backend normalization so default or empty OpenClaw config no longer adds `--permission-mode bypassPermissions`, while explicit full/off exec policy still does, and it also includes several gate-drift test/script/shrinkwrap edits.

PR surface: Source +22, Tests +160, Other -47. Total +135 across 21 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection reproduces the current-main behavior: `isOpenClawRequestedYolo()` defaults missing exec config to `full/off`, and the resolver-level test asserts default `bypassPermissions`. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers need to decide the Claude CLI default permission contract and resolve the blocked dependency graph changes before any automated repair would be safe.

target.title: fix(anthropic): stop default Claude permission bypass
target.body:
## Summary

- Stop defaulting Claude CLI runs to `--permission-mode bypassPermissions`; OpenClaw now only requests that mode when the explicit exec policy is full access with asks disabled.
- Preserve upstream Claude CLI safety defaults from `main`, including `--allowedTools mcp__openclaw__*`, `--setting-sources user`, native compaction ownership, and always-on resumed system prompt behavior.
- Refresh the branch against current `upstream/main` and repair local gate drift exposed by the merge, including Bash 3.2 Docker helper compatibility, provider auth alias test setup, compaction worker resolution, Chutes request header assertions, native hook bridge pruning tests, and gateway attachment cleanup retrying.

## Verification

Commands run:
- `CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `pnpm test:extension anthropic`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-support.config.ts`
- `pnpm check`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm test` (first full run exposed two suite-context failures; both were fixed)
- `pnpm test` (second full run passed all 88 Vitest shards in 669.79s)

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Claude CLI argv generation no longer injects `--permission-mode bypassPermissions` by default; it only adds that mode when OpenClaw receives an explicit YOLO exec policy.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout at PR head `3d897e8b83805cd8c7e534da8d295d0edf446b00` after merging official `upstream/main`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node --import tsx -e 'const { buildAnthropicCliBackend } = await import("./extensions/anthropic/cli-backend.ts"); const backend = buildAnthropicCliBackend(); const cases = [{name:"no context", ctx:undefined}, {name:"default empty OpenClaw config", ctx:{backendId:"claude-cli", config:{}}}, {name:"explicit YOLO exec", ctx:{backendId:"claude-cli", config:{tools:{exec:{security:"full", ask:"off"}}}}}, {name:"explicit restrictive exec", ctx:{backendId:"claude-cli", config:{tools:{exec:{security:"allowlist", ask:"on-miss"}}}}}]; for (const c of cases) { const cfg = backend.normalizeConfig ? backend.normalizeConfig(backend.config, c.ctx) : backend.config; const args = cfg.args ?? []; const resumeArgs = cfg.resumeArgs ?? []; console.log(`CASE ${c.name}`); console.log(`fresh.command=claude ${args.join(" ")}`); console.log(`fresh.permissionMode=${args.includes("--permission-mode") ? args[args.indexOf("--permission-mode") + 1] : "<omitted>"}`); console.log(`resume.permissionMode=${resumeArgs.includes("--permission-mode") ? resumeArgs[resumeArgs.indexOf("--permission-mode") + 1] : "<omitted>"}`); }'
```

- Evidence after fix (copied terminal output):

```text
CASE no context
fresh.command=claude -p --output-format stream-json --include-partial-messages --verbose --setting-sources user --allowedTools mcp__openclaw__*
fresh.permissionMode=<omitted>
resume.permissionMode=<omitted>
CASE default empty OpenClaw config
fresh.command=claude -p --output-format stream-json --include-partial-messages --verbose --setting-sources user --allowedTools mcp__openclaw__*
fresh.permissionMode=<omitted>
resume.permissionMode=<omitted>
CASE explicit YOLO exec
fresh.command=claude -p --output-format stream-json --include-partial-messages --verbose --setting-sources user --allowedTools mcp__openclaw__* --permission-mode bypassPermissions
fresh.permissionMode=bypassPermissions
resume.permissionMode=bypassPermissions
CASE explicit restrictive exec
fresh.command=claude -p --output-format stream-json --include-partial-messages --verbose --setting-sources user --allowedTools mcp__openclaw__*
fresh.permissionMode=<omitted>
resume.permissionMode=<omitted>
```

- Observed result after fix: Default and empty OpenClaw configs omit Claude `--permission-mode`; explicit `tools.exec.security: "full"` with `tools.exec.ask: "off"` adds `--permission-mode bypassPermissions`; restrictive exec policy omits the bypass. Existing safe Claude defaults stay present in the generated command: `--setting-sources user` and `--allowedTools mcp__openclaw__*`.
- What was not tested: A live Claude model/API request was not sent from this machine. The proof covers OpenClaw's real Anthropic backend argv normalization path immediately before Claude CLI process launch.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The command is local terminal output from the OpenClaw source checkout and redacts no secrets because none were printed.
target.author: plgonzalezrx8
